action.thought_summary: The initial requirement is broad - 'dynamic forms' could mean many things. I need to understand who the users are and what workflows they follow. Starting with user roles and primary use cases will ground the interview.
action.question: Who are the main users of this system? Will there be distinct roles like form creators who build the forms and form respondents who fill them out, or is it a single user type?

action.thought_summary: The user focuses on form creation and management. I need to probe what 'dynamic forms' means specifically—what field types, conditional logic, and validation capabilities they expect when building forms.
action.question: When you say 'dynamic forms,' what specific capabilities do you envision? For example, should form creators be able to add conditional logic (like showing certain fields only when a previous answer meets a condition), and what types of form fields do you need (text input, dropdowns, checkboxes, file uploads, date pickers, etc.)?

action.thought_summary: User mentioned editing field properties but was vague. Need to probe what properties they expect to configure for each field, as this is core to form customization.
action.question: When you edit a field's properties, what settings do you expect to configure? For example, should you be able to set field labels, placeholder text, whether a field is required or optional, validation rules (like min/max character length), and help text or descriptions for each field?

action.thought_summary: The user has been vague about specific features. I should probe the 'management' aspect of dynamic forms, which hasn't been explored yet - things like saving, publishing, organizing, and versioning forms.
action.question: What does 'managing' forms include for you? For example, should you be able to save forms as drafts and publish them later, organize forms into categories or folders, duplicate existing forms to create new ones, or track different versions of a form over time?

action.thought_summary: The user has been vague on many specifics but consistently points to form customization and preview as their focus. Preview hasn't been explored yet, so I should probe what they expect from the preview capability.
action.question: When you preview a form, what should the preview show you? For example, should it display the form exactly as a respondent would see and interact with it (allowing you to test filling it out), or is it more of a visual layout check where you just see how the form looks without being able to interact with the fields?

action.thought_summary: The user has been vague on specifics, consistently deferring detailed questions. They emphasize form creation, customization, and preview. I should probe a concrete interaction aspect of the form builder itself - how they envision the creation experience - to uncover implicit UI/UX requirements.
action.question: When building a form, how do you envision the creation interface? For example, should it be a drag-and-drop canvas where you drag field types onto the form, a sidebar where you add fields from a list, or something simpler like a step-by-step wizard that walks you through adding fields one at a time?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently unsure about detailed feature specifications. Rather than asking about specific features, I should probe about the end-to-end workflow - what happens after a form is finalized - to uncover implicit requirements about form distribution, sharing, or data collection that they may not have explicitly mentioned.
action.question: After you finalize a form, what do you expect to happen with it? For example, do you need to share or distribute the form to others (via a link, email, or embedding on a website), or is the form primarily for your own use within the system?

action.thought_summary: The user keeps redirecting to core features: login, form customization (add/remove fields, edit properties), and preview. Previous broad questions about field types, conditional logic, management features, and distribution haven't yielded specifics. I should try a more concrete, narrow question about a specific aspect of form customization they might have an opinion on, like field ordering or form structure elements.
action.question: When you add fields to a form, should you be able to reorder them (for example, moving a field from the bottom to the top of the form), or will fields simply appear in the order you add them?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently unsure about detailed feature questions, redirecting to their core focus of form creation, customization, and preview. I should try a different angle - asking about something more fundamental that they might be able to answer, like whether they need to create multiple forms or just one, and basic form identification.
action.question: Should you be able to create and manage multiple forms, or is this system for working with just a single form at a time? If multiple forms, how would you want to identify or distinguish them (for example, by giving each form a title or name)?

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently deferred on specific feature details but maintains core requirements: login, add/remove fields, edit field properties, preview, and finalize forms. I haven't yet explored the login/authentication aspect they mentioned, or what 'finalizing' entails. Let me probe the login and initial experience since they explicitly mentioned it.
action.question: You mentioned logging in as part of the workflow. Should the login be a simple username and password, or do you need other authentication methods like single sign-on or social login? Also, once logged in, what should you see first—a list of your existing forms or a blank form ready to edit?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently uncertain about specific feature details, always redirecting to their core focus of form creation, customization, and preview. Rather than continuing to ask about specific features they can't define, I should try to understand the purpose and context of the forms they're building, which may reveal implicit requirements about what the forms need to support.
action.question: What is the purpose or context of the forms you're creating? For example, are these forms for collecting customer information, internal business processes, surveys, registrations, or something else? Understanding what the forms will be used for may help clarify what features they need to support.

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently redirected to their core focus: logging in, customizing form structures (adding/removing fields, editing properties), and previewing before finalizing. Most specific feature questions have been met with uncertainty. I should probe what 'finalizing' a form means to them, as they've mentioned it multiple times but haven't defined it - this could reveal whether forms are locked, saved, published, or something else.
action.question: You've mentioned 'finalizing' forms a few times. What does finalizing a form mean to you? For example, does it mean you can no longer edit the form after finalizing it, or can you still go back and make changes? Also, should finalized forms be saved so you can return to them later?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently vague and unsure about most specific details. They keep redirecting to their core focus: login, add/remove fields, edit field properties, and preview forms. I haven't yet probed what 'dynamic' specifically means to them, which was their key term in the original requirement. This could reveal important implicit requirements about conditional logic, calculated fields, or data-driven form behavior.
action.question: You described these as 'dynamic forms' in your initial requirement. What makes the forms dynamic in your mind? For example, does 'dynamic' mean the form structure can change based on data (like fields appearing or hiding based on selections), does it mean the form content can be populated from an external data source, or does it simply mean you can modify the form structure yourself after creation?

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently been unable to provide specifics on most feature questions, reiterating only core needs: login, add/remove fields, edit field properties, and preview forms. I should try one more concrete question about the preview or editing experience before considering finishing, as the user may have limited requirements knowledge.
action.question: When you're editing a form's structure—adding, removing, or modifying fields—how should the editing view work? For example, should you see the form taking shape in real-time as you make changes (a live editing experience), or should you make changes in one area and then click a separate 'Preview' button to see the result?

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently been unsure about most specific feature details but keeps offering to address questions about form customization and preview features. I should try a more concrete, scenario-based question about the preview feature specifically, since that's one of their core stated requirements and they've offered to discuss it multiple times.
action.question: When you preview a form to see how it appears, should the preview show the form with sample data filled in so you can see what a completed form looks like, or should it show an empty form the way a respondent would first see it before filling anything out?

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently been unsure about most specific details, reiterating only core features: login, add/remove fields, edit field properties, and preview forms. I've covered many angles without getting specifics. Let me try one more concrete question about the basic field types they expect, since this is fundamental to the 'adding fields' functionality they keep mentioning.
action.question: When you add a field to a form, what are the most basic field types you would expect to have available? For example, at a minimum, would you need a text input field where users can type short answers, a larger text area for longer responses, and perhaps a dropdown to select from a list of options?

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently been unsure about most specific details, reiterating only core needs: login, add/remove fields, edit field properties, and preview before finalizing. After 19 turns with limited new information, I should try one more angle - perhaps about data persistence or visual styling - before finishing the interview.
action.question: Should the forms you create be saved persistently so they're still available when you log back in later, or is it acceptable if forms are only kept during your current session and lost when you log out?

action.thought_summary: After 19 turns, the user has consistently been unable to provide specific details beyond their core requirements. They keep redirecting to the same core features: login, add/remove fields, edit field properties, preview, and finalize. Most probing questions about specifics have been met with uncertainty. The turn budget is nearly exhausted, so it's time to finish and summarize what was elicited.
action.finish_summary: The user needs a website for creating and managing dynamic forms with the following core requirements identified: (1) Login functionality to access the system. (2) Form creation and customization by adding or removing fields. (3) Ability to edit field properties as part of customizing form structure. (4) Preview capability to see how forms appear before finalizing them. (5) A finalization step after previewing. The term 'dynamic' primarily refers to the flexible, modifiable nature of form structures rather than conditional logic or external data sources. Many specific details remain undefined by the user, including: specific field types, field property settings, conditional logic, form management features (drafts, publishing, categories, duplication, versioning), preview interactivity level, creation interface style (drag-and-drop vs wizard), post-finalization behavior, support for multiple forms, authentication method, landing page design, persistent storage requirements, and form distribution/sharing capabilities.
